macro_segments.title: The Debate on Precision (Tzimtzum) in Cases of Doubt: Bidei Shamayim vs. Bidei Adam

micro_segments.title: Introduction to Neima Ketannai: The Question of Whether Twins Born Simultaneously Can Both Be Bechor
The Two Interpretations of Bidei Adam: Construction vs. Measurement
The Case of the Dead Body Between Two Cities as Proof that Bidei Adam Allows Precision
Philosophical Analysis: Does Simultaneity Actually Occur in Physical Reality?
The Core Question: Can We Trust Our Measurements, or Is Perfect Precision Impossible?
Reconciling Halacha with Measurement: The Assumption of Error in Impossible Cases

macro_segments.title: Two Animals with Unknown Birth Order: Rabbi Tarfon vs. Rabbi Akiva on Distribution

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Tarfon's Position: The Kohen Picks the Nicer Animal
The Logic of Rabbi Tarfon: The Nicer Animal Was Likely Born First
Rabbi Akiva's Position: Meshamnim Beineihem (Split the Difference)
Rabbi Yochanan's Reinterpretation: Meshamnim Means the Difference in Value, Not Equal Partnership
The Problem of the Mishnah's Internal Contradiction Between Beginning and End
The One That Remains Must Be Guarded Until It Gets a Blemish; Obligation to Give Matanot

macro_segments.title: The Dispute Over Matanot: Rabbi Meir Obligates; Rabbi Yosei Exempts

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Meir's Position: The Kohen Can Claim from Both Sides (Mmah Nafshach)
Rabbi Yosei's Reasoning: The Sale Theory (Chalufin Beyad Kohen)
Rava's Analysis: The Concept of Asu She'eino Zocheh Kezocheh (Acting as if Acquired)
The Gemara Debate: Is Rabbi Yosei's Logic Really About Presumptive Sale or About Gezeirah?
Rabbi Elazar's Claim: Everyone Agrees That Without Exchange (Chalufin), Matanot Are Obligatory
The Gemara's Clarification: Rabbi Yosei's Reasoning Is Specifically Dependent on the Exchange

macro_segments.title: Comparison to Doubtful Ma'aser: Establishing the Principled Basis of the Dispute

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Observation: In Doubtful Ma'aser, Everyone Agrees the Kohen Gets Nothing
Why the Distinction: The Kohen Has No Legitimate Claim in Ma'aser Cases
The Gemara's Challenge: Is Rabbi Meir's Position Based on Protecting the Institution of Matanot?
The Gemara's Conclusion: The Debate Is on Principle (Al Pi Din), Not on Protective Measures

macro_segments.title: When One Animal Dies: Rabbi Tarfon Says Split (Yachloku) vs. Rabbi Akiva Says Hamotzi Mechavero

micro_segments.title: The Problem: Rabbi Tarfon's Position Changes from Picking the Nicer One to Splitting Equally
Rabbi Ami's Resolution: Rabbi Tarfon Must Have Reversed His Position (Chazar Bo)
Rabbi Akiva's Position: Hamotzi Mechavero Alav Hara'aya (Burden of Proof)
The Two Analogies: Shepherd with Third-Party Possession vs. One Person's Possession
Rav Pappa's Explanation: The Dispute Concerns a Kohen as Shepherd with Implicit Rights
Rabbi Tarfon's Theory: The Kohen Has Partial Possession Due to the Shepherd Arrangement
Rabbi Akiva's Counter: The Ba'al Habayit Does Not Implicitly Give Field Rights to the Kohen

macro_segments.title: Repetition of Cases with Two Different Animals: Variations on the Same Halachic Principles

micro_segments.title: Two Males: The Kohen Gets One; Two Females: The Kohen Gets One (Zachar and Nekeiva)
Three or Four: Uncertainty About Which Birth Order; The Kohen May Get Nothing
One Animal Previously Gave Birth, One Has Not: Birth Order Still Unknown
The Gemara's Question: Why Repeat These Cases If They All Follow the Same Principle?
Tzericha: Each Case Teaches That Strength/Healthiness Cannot Be Used as Proof of Birth Origin

topical_tags.term: Hamotzi Mechavero Alav Hara'aya (Burden of Proof)
Chalufin Beyad Kohen (Exchange in Kohen's Hand)
Asu She'eino Zocheh Kezocheh (Treating as if Acquired)
Dead Body Between Two Cities (Egla Arufa)
Al Pi Din (According to the Law)
